When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. ## Bottom line

**Qualified GO — but as a bootstrap vertical, not a venture-backed company.** The gap Rachel described is real and independently verified, but a semi-retired single founder cannot win "AI for all small firms" against Clio. What you *can* win: **compliance-first family law, starting in California, at $99–149/user/mo, sold through the community Rachel just came back from.** Realistic prize: 100–500 seats, $150k–800k ARR, sellable later. A parallel path worth exploring: licensing the compliance layer (policy engine + PII tokenization + citation verification) to a malpractice insurer or practice-management vendor.

The evidence in brief:

- **Demand is proven.** 71% of solos / 75% of small firms alrea


**User:** Instead of the externaly connected app would i be better off making apure mac app which only uses the api connection to anthropic ? Less moving parts to break


**Assistant:** Short answer: **you're onto something real, but "pure Mac app, zero backend" isn't quite achievable if you want to charge money — the right version of your idea is "local-first desktop app with one thin, stateless proxy," and it's arguably a *better* fit for the bootstrap-vertical path than the current architecture.** Here's the honest breakdown.

## What a local-first app genuinely wins

**A stronger confidentiality story than the web app has today.** Right now client data (tokenized) transits your Vercel functions and session state lives in Upstash Redis. In a desktop app, matters, chat history, token maps, and audit logs live only on the lawyer's machine; the sole egress is tokenized text to Anthropic under the DPA + no-training terms. That changes your legal posture as a *vendor*, not
